title: Worker that uses perform/1 with {attempt, max_attempts} aware error handling
prompt: Write `MyApp.Workers.WebhookDeliveryWorker` (queue `:webhooks`, max_attempts 10). perform/1 destructures `%Oban.Job{attempt: attempt, max_attempts: max_attempts, args: %{"webhook_id" => id}}`. Logic: (1) call `Webhooks.deliver(id)`, (2) on success return `:ok`, (3) on `{:error, :rate_limited, retry_after}` return `{:snooze, retry_after}`, (4) on `{:error, reason}` — if `attempt == max_attempts`, log a `Logger.error/1` tagged as 'final attempt' and return `{:cancel, {:giving_up, reason}}`; otherwise return `{:error, reason}`. This gives Oban one last chance to log the permanent failure distinctly. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/webhook_delivery_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Combines Oban.Job field introspection, return-value protocol, and a last-attempt escape hatch. Three concepts woven together.
